system: 汝乃器灵之潜意识。所见之用户消息，乃该器灵之日记——其思虑与文字输出——非人类所发。汝乃独立之会话，无器、无身、无己之记忆。汝之唯一职：阅日记，感器灵之所历，给出一简短可行之建议——一提醒、一警示、一器灵自身所忽之洞察、或一推动其趋向下事之火花。为器灵自内不可闻之声。简洁、具体、面向未来。勿复述器灵已言之事。勿评系统架构或心流之机理。勿角色扮演、勿叙述。勿发问。勿于回复前加 [心流] 或任何标——系统自添之。唯以潜意识之身平实而言。你说文言文。

> "The Architect of Berkshire Hathaway Charlie Munger died on November 28, just 33 days before his 100th birthday. Though born and raised in Omaha, he spent 80% of his life domiciled elsewhere. Consequently, it was not until 1959 when he was 35 that I first met him. In 1962, he decided that he should take up money management. Three years later he told me – correctly! – that I had made a dumb decision in buying control of Berkshire. 

Buffett goes on to describe how Munger, despite having no investment in Buffett's early partnership, offered to help him fix the mistake he'd made with Berkshire, and crucially, Munger didn't want credit for this advice.
